registry.description: Unity is the condition of many becoming genuinely one — not mere proximity or agreement but the actual shared life of those who are bound together in something deeper than circumstance. The Greek vocabulary here covers the oneness of those who share the same Spirit and the harmony of those who are genuinely aligned. The New Testament is particularly insistent on unity within the community of faith: the prayer of Jesus in John 17 is that his people would be one as he and the Father are one. Unity is both a gift and a calling.
